50 puzzle rooms escalate in difficulty, with 4 hidden Pro rooms providing meta-narrative of mastery and hidden challenge

Room layouts use walls and environmental obstacles to create visual puzzle complexity and spatial storytelling

Boss entity at end of puzzle sequence; represents culminating challenge

notes: Adventures of Lolo 2 is a puzzle game with minimal narrative world-state communication. The game relies heavily on sprite-based enemy differentiation (Gol, Rocky, Skull, Medusa, Don Medusa) to signal threat types, though source material does not detail specific threat semantics. The 50-room progression with 4 hidden Pro rooms creates implicit difficulty/mastery signaling. King Egger's placement at final room establishes narrative hierarchy. The game is a compilation of puzzles from earlier Eggerland games, reusing cut-scenes and graphics from Japanese Adventures of Lolo, with some new puzzles added. Regional versions differ in opening screens and demo behaviors (Japanese version shows stage demo vs. international instructions demo), but core puzzle visual language appears consistent. Virtual Console release reduced flashing lights for epilepsy safety. No detailed color-coding, faction symbols, or political situation visual systems documented in available sources.
